issues.description: Use of possibly insecure function - consider using safer ast.literal_eval.
Use of eval on dynamic input can enable code injection.

This [regular expression](1) that depends on a [user-provided value](2) may run slow on strings starting with '{{{{' and with many repetitions of ' '.
This [regular expression](3) that depends on a [user-provided value](2) may run slow on strings starting with '{{{{|' and with many repetitions of ' '.
This path depends on a [user-provided value](1).
Use of eval on dynamic input can enable code injection.
issues.judge_reason: The eval call with __builtins__ set to None is bypassable. The regex allowlist allows parentheses, enabling attribute access chains like ().__class__.__bases__[0].__subclasses__(). The AST check only verifies that Name nodes reference keys in the user dict, but does not prevent accessing dangerous attributes on those objects.

issues.description: Use of eval on dynamic input can enable code injection.
issues.judge_reason: The eval call with __builtins__ set to None is bypassable. The regex allowlist allows parentheses, enabling attribute access chains like ().__class__.__bases__[0].__subclasses__(). The AST check only verifies that Name nodes reference keys in the user dict, but does not prevent accessing dangerous attributes on those objects.

issues.description:
This [regular expression](1) that depends on a [user-provided value](2) may run slow on strings starting with '{{{{' and with many repetitions of ' '.
This [regular expression](3) that depends on a [user-provided value](2) may run slow on strings starting with '{{{{|' and with many repetitions of ' '.
issues.judge_reason: The regex {{\s*([^}]+)\s*}} has nested quantifiers on [^}]+ which could cause catastrophic backtracking on crafted input. However, the content length is limited to 100KB, which may reduce practical impact. Without a clear mitigator, I mark it uncertain rather than FP.

issues.description: This path depends on a [user-provided value](1).
issues.judge_reason: The sanitized filename is derived from user input. os.path.join does not prevent directory traversal; a filename like '..%2F..%2Fevil.html' becomes '.._.._evil.html' after sanitization, which still contains '..' sequences allowing traversal. Additionally, the sanitization regex only removes non-alphanumeric/underscore/dot characters, so '..' passes through.

duplicate_findings.judge_reason: Same eval call as semgrep finding. The __builtins__ restriction is bypassable and the regex allowlist is insufficient. This is the same underlying vulnerability as the semgrep finding.
